text.title: domain-driven-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the domain-driven-design methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Order has only getters/setters; all logic in OrderService; same rule duplicated in CLI service and event handler.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated entity as data holder.
antipattern.fix: Move logic into the entity; service becomes a thin orchestration layer.

antipattern.symptom: Order(BaseModel) inherits from SQLAlchemy declarative base; tests now need a DB.
antipattern.fix: Define plain Order in domain/; map to persistence in infrastructure/.

antipattern.symptom: Two services name a 'Customer' but mean different things; one update silently corrupts the other.
antipattern.fix: Author docs/context-map.md; add anti-corruption layers where contexts touch.

antipattern.symptom: Money has set_amount(); two references mutate the same Money, accounting drifts.
antipattern.fix: Make Money immutable; expose with_amount() returning a new instance.
